
---
title: "Executive Summary"
---


## Executive Summary

This report compares workplace injury risk between manufacturing workers and office workers (finance, insurance, and professional services) using 2023–2024 data from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. The analysis covers both nonfatal injuries and workplace deaths.

Key findings:

- **Manufacturing workers are injured at a much higher rate overall** — 86.4 injuries per 10,000 workers, compared to 11.0 in finance and insurance and 18.6 in professional services.
- **Falls make up a disproportionately large share of injuries in office sectors.** Falls account for 33.6% of all injuries in finance and insurance, compared to 21.6% in manufacturing.
- **The same pattern holds for workplace deaths.** Falls caused 33.3% of deaths in finance and insurance, versus 14.7% in manufacturing.
- **Recommendation:** Office environments should prioritize fall prevention programs even though overall injury rates are low. Falls are the dominant injury risk in these settings.

---
